summary.headline: AEM new RED — Q2 2026 gold crash -14% ($4,861→$4,040/oz, steepest quarterly decline on record) directly matches thesis key_risk #1; escalated AMBER→RED. MARA/HUT new AMBER on crypto-sector weakness and Q1 EPS miss. STLD new AMBER on Q2 guidance miss and margin compression. LRCX recovery (+4.88%) on Micron blowout — downgraded RED→AMBER. KEYS stabilizing — downgraded RED→AMBER. CAT tariff risk improving — resolved GREEN. ADI fully recovered — resolved GREEN. MRVL/CRDO/QCOM persisting RED. Hyperscaler late-July earnings window (MSFT July 29) remains primary macro catalyst for 8 AI-infrastructure holdings.

flags.headline: MRVL -3.68% (cumulative -9.2% from June 26): AI capex moderation persisting — CFO insider sale at 91x P/E unresolved
flags.detail: Marvell Technology continues to slide — $265.84 June 26 close → $256.06 June 29 close (-3.68% session). Cumulative decline is now ~9.2% from the June 26 pre-sell-off level. The CFO's insider sale of 211,000 shares at 91x trailing P/E remains a concern alongside the SK Hynix HBM4 ramp delay. Custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium) and 1.6T networking thesis remains intact operationally, but near-term capex moderation risk is materializing. No analyst downgrades yet; consensus remains bullish (Strong Buy). Watching MSFT July 29 capex commentary closely.
flags.thesis_link: Matches MRVL key_risk #3: 'AI capex cycle moderation — if hyperscalers reduce data center investment due to ROI concerns or macro headwinds, Marvell's growth trajectory toward $15B revenue by FY2028 would be at risk.' Severity escalated AMBER → RED per thesis-match protocol on June 26 flag; persisting.

evidence.source: CNBC — Jensen Huang calls MRVL 'next trillion-dollar company' June 2
Timothy Sykes — MRVL CFO sale and chip weakness
ZeroHedge — Korean article that sparked the global chip selloff

flags.headline: CRDO -2.06% (cumulative -22% from all-time high June 22): AI capex cycle risk persisting
flags.detail: Credo Technology fell another -2.06% on June 29 ($239.63→$234.70), now ~22% below its June 22 all-time high of $308.67. The sell-off was triggered by SK Hynix HBM4 production slowdown (strategic redirect to conventional DRAM) and Broadcom post-earnings capex guidance miss. HBM4 ramp pushed Q2→Q3. The -2.06% session move vs the -10.5% initial sell-off shows the acute phase has passed — but the cumulative drawdown and AI capex sensitivity warrant keeping RED. Analyst community remains bullish: BofA $340 Buy, Stifel $350. Consensus Strong Buy (18 analysts, 0 Sell). Watching hyperscaler capex commentary July 29+.
flags.thesis_link: Matches CRDO key_risk #3: 'AI capex cycle dependency — a slowdown in hyperscaler data center spending due to ROI concerns or macroeconomic headwinds would directly reduce demand for Credo's products given near-total AI exposure.' The SK Hynix HBM4 slowdown and Broadcom capex miss are direct proxies for this risk materializing.

evidence.source: TechPowerUp — SK Hynix slows HBM4 ramp
ZeroHedge — Korean article that sparked the global chip selloff
MarketBeat CRDO — analyst consensus Strong Buy

flags.headline: QCOM -0.52% — memory shortage persisting, June worst month in 7 years (-18% MTD), CEO warns into FY2027-28
flags.detail: Qualcomm continues to drift lower (-0.52% June 29, $188.61→$187.63), now -18% for June — on track for the worst month in approximately 7 years. No bounce has materialized from the June 26 -8.0% drop. CEO Cristiano Amon directly cited the global memory shortage as constraining handset production and warned it could persist into FY2027-28. Apple modem displacement risk (est. 20-25% of QCT chip revenue) remains a separate long-term overhang. Analyst consensus mixed: Benchmark Buy $300, RBC Hold $250, BofA Underperform $220. Not a BUY name until memory clarity improves.
flags.thesis_link: Matches QCOM key_risk #1: 'Global memory shortage constraining handset production — Q2 FY2026 guidance cut reflects supply limitations that CEO Amon warns could persist into FY2027-28, compressing near-term revenue growth.'

evidence.source: StockAnalysis QCOM — price and financials
Saxo Market Quick Take June 29

flags.headline: AEM -3.02%: Q2 2026 gold crash -14% ($4,861→$4,040/oz) matches thesis key_risk #1 — escalated AMBER→RED
flags.detail: Agnico Eagle Mines fell -3.02% on June 29 ($157.12→$152.43), reflecting the Q2 2026 gold price collapse — the steepest quarterly decline on record, from ~$4,861/oz (Q1 peak) to ~$4,040/oz (-16.9%). This directly matches AEM's key_risk #1 in the June 30 report: 'Gold price reversion from current near-record levels (~$4,800+/oz) to historical averages would significantly compress margins.' AEM's AISC is $1,339/oz (industry-leading), so at $4,040/oz there remains a healthy $2,701/oz spread — production economics are intact. However, the $820/oz price decline from the report's reference level compresses operating leverage significantly. Morgan Stanley projects gold could find support at $3,800-4,000/oz range near-term. Thesis severity escalated AMBER→RED per protocol.
flags.thesis_link: Matches AEM key_risk #1: 'Gold price reversion from current near-record levels (~$4,800+/oz) to historical averages would significantly compress margins — at $2,000/oz gold, operating margins would shrink dramatically despite industry-leading AISC of $1,339/oz.' Q2 2026 gold dropped -14% — steepest quarterly decline on record. Severity escalated AMBER → RED per thesis-match protocol.

evidence.source: Reuters — Gold posts steepest quarterly drop on record in Q2 2026
AEM Q1 FY2026 Earnings Release — AISC $1,339/oz, record production
Yahoo Finance AEM — key statistics and price history

flags.headline: MARA -5.08%: Q1 2026 EPS miss ($-3.31 vs $-1.51 consensus); Bernstein cuts PT $23→$17; Morgan Stanley Underweight $7
flags.detail: Marathon Digital Holdings fell -5.08% on June 29 ($14.45→$13.715) on mounting analyst concern following Q1 2026 EPS of -$3.31 vs. consensus of -$1.51 — a miss of $1.80/share (119%). Bernstein cut price target $23→$17, maintaining Underperform. Morgan Stanley rates Underweight with a $7 target. Negative FCF of -$327.5M in Q1 reflects elevated energy costs, Bitcoin price softness, and heavy CapEx from mining expansion. Bitcoin is under pressure from Federal Reserve hawkishness and energy-cost headwinds. A WATCH name (score 43) — the thesis is leveraged entirely to Bitcoin price recovery.
flags.thesis_link: Matches MARA key_risks: 'Bitcoin price dependency/fragility — MARA's entire revenue and profitability hinge on Bitcoin price; sustained Bitcoin weakness compresses margins to near-zero while fixed mining costs persist.' Q1 EPS miss confirms Bitcoin fragility risk materializing.

evidence.source: Bernstein — MARA price target cut $23→$17, Underperform
Morgan Stanley — MARA Underweight, $7 target
MARA Q1 2026 Earnings Release — EPS -$3.31, FCF -$327.5M

flags.headline: HUT -4.52%: Crypto-sector weakness; $2.35M Bitcoin-merger settlement; new board chair
flags.detail: Hut 8 Corp fell -4.52% on June 29 ($122.46→$116.92) on broad crypto-sector weakness. Company-specific developments: (1) $2.35M settlement related to the 2023 merger with US Bitcoin Corp — not material to thesis but adds legal overhang, (2) new board chair appointment (signal of governance transition during River Bend data center buildout). The River Bend execution risk remains the primary watch item; sustained cash burn (-$X monthly) during construction will pressure balance sheet if Bitcoin doesn't recover. HOLD name (score 61). No immediate action warranted but monitoring closely.
flags.thesis_link: Matches HUT key_risks: 'Execution risk on River Bend data center buildout — delays or cost overruns would extend negative FCF period'; 'Bitcoin price/regulatory risk — sustained Bitcoin weakness compresses mining economics and delays FCF breakeven.' Sector weakness amplifying company-specific transition risk.

evidence.source: Yahoo Finance HUT — price and recent news
Business Wire / Globe Newswire — Hut 8 settlement and board update

flags.headline: STLD -3.29%: Q2 EPS guidance miss; -9.97% over 30 days; margin compression matches thesis key_risk
flags.detail: Steel Dynamics fell -3.29% on June 29 ($245.53→$237.47), extending a -9.97% 30-day decline. Q2 2026 EPS guidance came in below consensus, reflecting steel price cyclicality compressing margins — spot HRC steel prices have softened following tariff-driven demand pull-forward. STLD reports in mid-July; Q2 results will confirm whether the margin compression is transitory or deepening. Currently trading below both 50-day and 200-day MAs. HOLD name (score 72). The thesis (premium mini-mill efficiency, diversified steel mix) remains intact, but cyclical margin pressure warrants monitoring.
flags.thesis_link: Matches STLD key_risk #1: 'Steel price cyclicality — profit margin compressed to 6.5%; if HRC prices decline further from current levels, Steel Dynamics' earnings would face meaningful compression, potentially pressuring the stock's premium multiple.' Q2 guidance miss confirms margin compression materializing.

evidence.source: Yahoo Finance STLD — price, news, and guidance
Steel Benchmark HRC — spot price tracker

flags.headline: LRCX +4.88%: Recovering strongly on Micron Q3 blowout — downgraded RED→AMBER; WFE demand intact
flags.detail: Lam Research surged +4.88% on June 29 ($377.46→$395.86) on Micron's blowout Q3 FY2026 results — DRAM and NAND demand remains strong, partially offsetting the SK Hynix HBM4 ramp delay concern. BofA raised LRCX price target to $480 (Buy). The WFE cyclicality risk flag remains valid (thesis cross-check still holds), but the acute phase of the June 26 sell-off has reversed. Downgraded from RED to AMBER pending hyperscaler capex commentary (MSFT July 29). LRCX is still ~3.4% below its June 22 all-time high.
flags.thesis_link: Matches LRCX key_risk #1: 'Semiconductor equipment cyclicality — WFE spending has historically experienced 20-30% peak-to-trough swings.' Risk remains valid; Micron recovery softens near-term concern. Severity downgraded RED → AMBER on recovery — monitoring ahead of hyperscaler earnings.

evidence.source: BofA — LRCX price target raised to $480, Buy
Micron Q3 FY2026 earnings — DRAM/NAND demand strong

flags.headline: KEYS -0.27%: Stabilizing after June 26 -7.64% drop; valuation concern persists at elevated multiple
flags.detail: Keysight Technologies is stabilizing (-0.27% June 29, $332.61→$331.72) after the -7.64% June 26 drop. No additional negative catalysts. The underlying concern — high valuation (51x trailing P/E) + AI capex dependency — remains but is not acutely worsening. Q2 FY2026 showed 31% AI-segment revenue growth; operational thesis intact. The sell-off appears to have been profit-taking after KEYS ran +99.3% YoY. Downgraded from RED to AMBER: watching for valuation compression to stabilize or any revenue guidance changes.
flags.thesis_link: Matches KEYS key_risks: (1) 'AI capex cycle dependency'; (2) 'Valuation compression risk — at 51.2x trailing P/E, KEYS requires continued 20%+ growth.' Valuation concern persists; downgraded RED → AMBER on stabilization.

evidence.source: GuruFocus — KEYS shares stabilizing, still flagged overvalued

flags.headline: NXPI -1.24%: Automotive chip softness persisting; recovery slower than peers
flags.detail: NXP Semiconductors fell -1.24% on June 29 ($275.97→$272.55), lagging the partial recovery seen in other semis (LRCX, ADI). NXPI's automotive-heavy mix means it has less AI capex exposure than peers, but automotive chip demand remains soft. Q1 2026 revenue guided up 11% YoY — automotive recovery thesis intact but the pace is slower than consensus expected. HOLD name (score 65). No new negative catalyst; monitoring for Q2 guidance update.

flags.headline: Late-July hyperscaler earnings — capex commentary is highest-priority macro signal for 8 AI-infra holdings
flags.detail: Microsoft Q4 FY2026 earnings confirmed July 29 — first hyperscaler read on 2H 2026 data center capex. Amazon, Alphabet, and Meta Q2 2026 dates expected late July/early August. Together these four hyperscalers pledged $750B+ in collective 2026 capex. Any revision to data center spending guidance will directly move CRDO, MRVL, AMD, NVDA, LRCX, KEYS, TSM, and ADI (8 of 50 active holdings = 16% of portfolio). MSFT July 29 is the first and most important read. 29 days to event.

evidence.source: Microsoft Investor Relations — Q4 FY2026 earnings call July 29

flags.headline: CAT +0.86%: Tariff headwind resolving — estimate revised down to $2.2-2.4B; price recovering — resolved GREEN
flags.detail: Caterpillar is recovering +0.86% on June 29 (~$1,006.77) after the June 26 -5.53% sell-off. White House tariff reduction announcements have brought CAT's own cost estimate DOWN from $2.6B to $2.2-2.4B. Q1 2026: 22% revenue surge, $63B backlog. The thesis risk is partially resolving. Removing from active flag list — no further monitoring needed unless tariff situation reverses.

evidence.source: GuruFocus — CAT shares surge following tariff reductions

flags.headline: ADI +1.67%: Fully recovering from June 26 sector contagion — resolved GREEN
flags.detail: Analog Devices is recovering +1.67% on June 29 ($384.61→$391.03) — fully recovering from the June 26 -8.0% sector contagion drop. No company-specific negative catalyst was ever identified. Q2 FY2026 guidance of $3.5B with ~47.5% adjusted operating margins intact. Data center revenue +50% in FY2025. Thesis intact. HOLD name (score 78). Removing from active flag list.
